bpmn_config_generation_guide.overview:
This guide provides comprehensive instructions for generating BPMN process configurations from business blueprint documents. The config-driven approach separates GenAI's document understanding capabilities from deterministic BPMN XML generation.
**Key Principle**: GenAI analyzes documents and creates JSON configs; Python generates BPMN XML.

separation_of_concerns.genai_role: - Analyze business documents - Extract process information (roles, steps, decisions, flows) - Understand business context and intent - Create structured JSON configurations - Handle ambiguity and incomplete information
separation_of_concerns.python_role: - Read JSON configurations - Generate valid BPMN 2.0 XML - Ensure proper element references - Apply BAW-specific conventions - Validate XML structure

config_schema_reference.description: Complete JSON schema definition with 4 example configs covering all BPMN patterns: - Linear flow (simple sequential process) - Approval flow (with decision gateways) - Parallel flow (concurrent activities) - Swimlane flow (multi-role process)
config_schema_reference.important: Always reference CONFIG_SCHEMA_DESIGN.md for the authoritative schema structure. The examples in that file show the exact JSON format expected by the Python generator.

step.title: Identify Process Scope
step.description: Determine which processes are described in the blueprint document

actions.action: Scan for process names, workflow descriptions, or procedure titles
Identify process boundaries (start and end points)

step.title: Identify Roles and Actors
step.description: Extract all participants in the process

actions.action: Identify system actors (Automated System, External API)
Assign unique role IDs using role-[name] pattern

step.title: Identify Milestones
step.description: Extract key process milestones or phases

actions.action: Look for major process phases or stages
Identify completion criteria for each milestone
Assign unique milestone IDs using ms-[name] pattern

step.title: Extract Process Elements
step.description: Identify all BPMN elements (events, tasks, gateways)

extraction_tips.tip: Look for action verbs (Submit, Review, Approve, Process)
Identify who performs the action (determines assignee)
Note if action is automated (serviceTask) or manual (userTask)
Look for decision points, questions, or conditional logic
Identify conditions for each outgoing path

actions.action: Identify flow sequence from process description
Assign unique flow IDs using flow-[number] pattern
Connect elements using sourceRef and targetRef
Add conditions for gateway outgoing flows

validation_rules.rule: Every element must have at least one incoming or outgoing flow (except start/end)
Start events have only outgoing flows
End events have only incoming flows
Gateway outgoing flows should have conditions (except default)
All flow references must point to existing element IDs

when_to_use.use_case: Visual separation of responsibilities is important
Process spans multiple departments or systems

lane_structure.field: Array of element IDs in this lane

assignment_rules.rule: Assign tasks to lanes based on assignee role
Gateways typically go in the lane of the preceding task
Start/end events can be in any appropriate lane

id_conventions.overview: All elements must have unique, predictable IDs for proper XML referencing. Use these patterns consistently across all configs.

numbering_guidelines.guideline: Start numbering at 1 for each element type
Maintain number sequence even if elements are reordered
Don't reuse numbers within the same config

example.name: simple_linear_process
example.description: Basic sequential process with no branching
example.blueprint_excerpt: "The application process starts when an agent submits an application. The system validates the data, then routes it to an underwriter for review. After review, the underwriter makes a decision and the process ends."

example.name: approval_with_gateway
example.description: Process with decision gateway and conditional flows
example.blueprint_excerpt: "After validation, the system checks if the application is complete. If complete, it routes to underwriter. If incomplete, it returns to agent. The underwriter reviews and either approves or declines."

category.name: structure
category.check: Config has all required top-level sections (process, roles, elements, flows)
All required fields present for each element type

category.name: references
category.check: All flow sourceRef values reference existing element IDs
All flow targetRef values reference existing element IDs
All task assignee values reference existing role IDs
All lane flowNodeRefs reference existing element IDs

category.name: flow_logic
category.check: Start events have only outgoing flows
End events have only incoming flows
All tasks have at least one incoming and one outgoing flow
Gateway outgoing flows have conditions (except default path)
All paths lead to an end event

category.name: business_logic
category.check: Process name and description are clear and meaningful
Role assignments make sense for task types
Gateway conditions are mutually exclusive (for exclusiveGateway)

integration_with_python.overview: After generating the JSON config, it will be consumed by Python modules in BPMN_tools/ to generate valid BPMN XML.

python_modules.module: Main tool for BPMN XML generation from config

usage_pattern.step: 1. Generate JSON config in this mode
3. Automatically run generate_bpmn.py to create BPMN XML
4. BPMN XML is saved to business-processes/bpmn/[context]/
5. BPMN XML is ready for BAW deployment

integration_with_python.important: This mode generates BPMN config files AND automatically runs the Python generator (generate_bpmn.py) to create BPMN XML files. This is handled in Step 8 of the workflow. The generated BPMN XML files are immediately ready for packaging or deployment.

pattern.name: linear_sequence
pattern.when: Simple sequential process with no branching

pattern.name: multi_role_swimlane
pattern.when: Process spans multiple departments or roles
pattern.structure: Use lanes array to group elements by role
pattern.example_ref: See lanes section in simple_linear_process example

issue.name: missing_flows
issue.symptom: Element has no incoming or outgoing flows
issue.solution: Add flows connecting element to process flow

issue.name: invalid_references
issue.solution: Verify all sourceRef and targetRef values match element IDs

issue.name: gateway_without_conditions
issue.symptom: Exclusive gateway outgoing flows lack conditions
issue.solution: Add conditionExpression to each outgoing flow (except default)

issue.name: orphaned_elements
issue.symptom: Element not reachable from start event
issue.solution: Add flows connecting element to main process flow

best_practices.practice: Start with process metadata and roles before extracting elements
Number elements sequentially as you extract them from the document
Use descriptive names for elements and flows
Add documentation fields to capture business context
Validate config structure before considering it complete
Keep configs focused on single processes (avoid mega-processes)
Use milestones to mark significant process phases
